1	"""Notification & in-app messaging."""
2	from __future__ import annotations
3	import json
4	import re
5	from datetime import datetime, timedelta
6	
7	from .. import audit, config, db, events
8	from ..models import NotificationMessage
9	from ..permissions import Session, requires
10	from .auth import BizError
11	
12	
13	_VAR_RE = re.compile(r"\{([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\}")
14	
15	
16	class NotificationService:
17	
18	    # ---- templates --------------------------------------------------------
19	
20	    def list_templates(self, session: Session) -> list[dict]:
21	        conn = db.get_connection()
22	        return [dict(r) for r in conn.execute(
23	            "SELECT id, name, subject, body, variables_json FROM notif_templates "
24	            "ORDER BY name")]
25	
26	    @requires("notification.admin")
27	    def upsert_template(self, session: Session, name: str, subject: str,
28	                        body: str) -> int:
29	        variables = sorted(set(_VAR_RE.findall(subject + " " + body)))
30	        unknown = [v for v in variables if v not in config.TEMPLATE_VARIABLES]
31	        if unknown:
32	            audit.record(session.user_id, "notif_template", name,
33	                         "warn_unknown_vars", {"unknown": unknown})
34	        with db.transaction() as conn:
35	            existing = conn.execute(
36	                "SELECT id FROM notif_templates WHERE name=?", (name,)).fetchone()
37	            vj = json.dumps(variables)
38	            if existing:
39	                conn.execute(
40	                    "UPDATE notif_templates SET subject=?, body=?, variables_json=? "
41	                    "WHERE id=?", (subject, body, vj, existing["id"]))
42	                tid = existing["id"]
43	            else:
44	                cur = conn.execute(
45	                    "INSERT INTO notif_templates(name, subject, body, variables_json) "
46	                    "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)", (name, subject, body, vj))
47	                tid = cur.lastrowid
48	        audit.record(session.user_id, "notif_template", tid, "upsert",
49	                     {"name": name})
50	        return tid
51	
52	    # ---- rules ------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	    def list_rules(self, session: Session) -> list[dict]:
55	        conn = db.get_connection()
56	        return [dict(r) for r in conn.execute(
57	            "SELECT id, name, kind, event_name, cron_spec, template_id, "
58	            "audience_query, enabled FROM notif_rules ORDER BY name")]
59	
60	    @requires("notification.admin")
61	    def set_rule_enabled(self, session: Session, rule_id: int, enabled: bool) -> None:
62	        with db.transaction() as conn:
63	            conn.execute("UPDATE notif_rules SET enabled=? WHERE id=?",
64	                         (1 if enabled else 0, rule_id))
65	        audit.record(session.user_id, "notif_rule", rule_id, "set_enabled",
66	                     {"enabled": enabled})
67	
68	    # ---- direct enqueue --------------------------------------------------
69	
70	    def enqueue(self, session: Session, *, template_name: str,
71	                audience_user_ids: list[int], variables: dict[str, str],
72	                scheduled_for: datetime | None = None) -> list[int]:
73	        conn = db.get_connection()
74	        tpl = conn.execute(
75	            "SELECT id, subject, body FROM notif_templates WHERE name=?",
76	            (template_name,)).fetchone()
77	        if not tpl:
78	            raise BizError("TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND", "Unknown template.")
79	        subject = render(tpl["subject"], variables)
80	        body = render(tpl["body"], variables)
81	        msg_ids: list[int] = []
82	        with db.transaction() as conn:
83	            for uid in audience_user_ids:
84	                cur = conn.execute(
85	                    """INSERT INTO notif_messages(template_id, audience_user_id,
86	                          subject, body_rendered, status, scheduled_for)
87	                       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'queued', ?)""",
88	                    (tpl["id"], uid, subject, body,
89	                     scheduled_for.isoformat() if scheduled_for else None))
90	                msg_ids.append(cur.lastrowid)
91	        return msg_ids
92	
93	    # ---- inbox ------------------------------------------------------------
94	
95	    def inbox(self, session: Session, *, only_unread: bool = False,
96	              limit: int = 100) -> list[NotificationMessage]:
97	        conn = db.get_connection()
98	        sql = """SELECT m.id, t.name AS tname, m.subject, m.body_rendered,
99	                         m.status, m.attempts, m.scheduled_for, m.created_at, m.read_at
100	                  FROM notif_messages m JOIN notif_templates t ON t.id = m.template_id
101	                  WHERE m.audience_user_id = ? AND m.status='delivered'"""
102	        args = [session.user_id]
103	        if only_unread:
104	            sql += " AND m.read_at IS NULL"
105	        sql += " ORDER BY m.created_at DESC LIMIT ?"
106	        args.append(limit)
107	        rows = conn.execute(sql, args).fetchall()
108	        return [NotificationMessage(
109	            id=r["id"], template_name=r["tname"], subject=r["subject"],
110	            body=r["body_rendered"], status=r["status"], attempts=r["attempts"],
111	            scheduled_for=r["scheduled_for"], created_at=r["created_at"],
112	            read_at=r["read_at"]) for r in rows]
113	
114	    def unread_count(self, session: Session) -> int:
115	        conn = db.get_connection()
116	        return conn.execute(
117	            "SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM notif_messages WHERE audience_user_id=? "
118	            "AND status='delivered' AND read_at IS NULL",
119	            (session.user_id,)).fetchone()["n"]
120	
121	    def mark_read(self, session: Session, message_id: int) -> None:
122	        with db.transaction() as conn:
123	            conn.execute(
124	                "UPDATE notif_messages SET read_at=datetime('now') "
125	                "WHERE id=? AND audience_user_id=?",
126	                (message_id, session.user_id))
127	
128	    # ---- dispatcher (called by worker) -----------------------------------
129	
130	    def drain_queue(self) -> int:
131	        """Deliver due messages. Returns number delivered.
132	
133	        Side-effect: any ``notif_enqueue_failures`` rows whose 5-minute retry
134	        window has elapsed are auto-replayed before draining, so the standard
135	        cadence applies even without an operator-driven retry.
136	        """
137	        self._auto_replay_failures()
138	        conn = db.get_connection()
139	        now_iso = datetime.utcnow().isoformat(timespec="seconds")
140	        rows = conn.execute(
141	            "SELECT id FROM notif_messages "
142	            "WHERE status='queued' AND (scheduled_for IS NULL OR scheduled_for <= ?)",
143	            (now_iso,)).fetchall()
144	        delivered = 0
145	        for r in rows:
146	            try:
147	                with db.transaction() as conn:
148	                    conn.execute(
149	                        "UPDATE notif_messages SET status='delivered', "
150	                        "attempts=attempts+1, last_attempt_at=datetime('now') "
151	                        "WHERE id=?", (r["id"],))
152	                delivered += 1
153	            except Exception:
154	                self._handle_failure(r["id"])
155	        return delivered
156	
157	    def _handle_failure(self, msg_id: int) -> None:
158	        conn = db.get_connection()
159	        row = conn.execute(
160	            "SELECT attempts FROM notif_messages WHERE id=?", (msg_id,)).fetchone()
161	        if not row:
162	            return
163	        attempts = row["attempts"] + 1
164	        next_status = "dead" if attempts >= config.NOTIF_RETRY_LIMIT else "queued"
165	        next_for = (datetime.utcnow()
166	                    + timedelta(seconds=config.NOTIF_RETRY_INTERVAL_SECONDS)).isoformat()
167	        try:
168	            with db.transaction() as conn:
169	                conn.execute(
170	                    "UPDATE notif_messages SET attempts=?, status=?, "
171	                    "scheduled_for=?, last_attempt_at=datetime('now') WHERE id=?",
172	                    (attempts, next_status, next_for, msg_id))
173	        except Exception:
174	            pass
175	
176	    def fire_scheduled_rules(self, now: datetime | None = None) -> int:
177	        """Evaluate all `kind='schedule'` rules whose cron spec matches *now*.
178	
179	        Subset cron grammar: ``M H * * *`` (minute, hour, *, *, *) — fires
180	        once per matching minute. Last-fire timestamp lives in
181	        `notif_rule_runs` so a tick that lands inside the same minute does
182	        not re-fire. Returns number of rules fired.
183	        """
184	        ts = (now or datetime.now()).replace(second=0, microsecond=0)
185	        conn = db.get_connection()
186	        rows = conn.execute(
187	            "SELECT id, name, cron_spec, template_id, audience_query "
188	            "FROM notif_rules WHERE enabled=1 AND kind='schedule'").fetchall()
189	        fired = 0
190	        for r in rows:
191	            if not _cron_matches(r["cron_spec"], ts):
192	                continue
193	            last = conn.execute(
194	                "SELECT last_fired_at FROM notif_rule_runs WHERE rule_id=?",
195	                (r["id"],)).fetchone()
196	            this_min = ts.isoformat(timespec="minutes")
197	            if last and last["last_fired_at"] == this_min:
198	                continue
199	            audience = _resolve_audience(json.loads(r["audience_query"] or "{}"))
200	            if not audience:
201	                continue
202	            tpl_subject = _template_subject(r["template_id"])
203	            tpl_body = _template_body(r["template_id"])
204	            vars_ = {"Today": ts.strftime("%m/%d/%Y")}
205	            with db.transaction() as conn:
206	                for uid in audience:
207	                    conn.execute(
208	                        """INSERT INTO notif_messages(template_id, audience_user_id,
209	                              subject, body_rendered, status)
210	                           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'queued')""",
211	                        (r["template_id"], uid,
212	                         render(tpl_subject, vars_), render(tpl_body, vars_)))
213	                conn.execute(
214	                    "INSERT INTO notif_rule_runs(rule_id, last_fired_at) "
215	                    "VALUES (?, ?) ON CONFLICT(rule_id) DO UPDATE SET "
216	                    "last_fired_at=excluded.last_fired_at",
217	                    (r["id"], this_min))
218	            fired += 1
219	        return fired
220	
221	    def retry_failed(self, session: Session) -> int:
222	        """Re-queue dead-letter rows AND replay any enqueue-stage failures."""
223	        with db.transaction() as conn:
224	            cur = conn.execute(
225	                "UPDATE notif_messages SET status='queued', attempts=0, "
226	                "scheduled_for=NULL WHERE status='dead'")
227	            requeued = cur.rowcount
228	        # Operator-triggered retry replays ALL outstanding enqueue failures
229	        # immediately (regardless of the 5-minute window).
230	        replayed = self._replay_enqueue_failures(force=True)
231	        return requeued + replayed
232	
233	    def _auto_replay_failures(self) -> int:
234	        """Auto-replay only enqueue failures whose 5-minute window elapsed."""
235	        return self._replay_enqueue_failures(force=False)
236	
237	    # Hard cap on enqueue-failure replay attempts. Matches the
238	    # delivery-side 3-attempt budget documented in design.md §7 so a
239	    # permanently-broken row is moved to a dead state instead of being
240	    # retried indefinitely on every dispatcher tick.
241	    _ENQUEUE_REPLAY_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3
242	
243	    def _replay_enqueue_failures(self, *, force: bool) -> int:
244	        conn = db.get_connection()
245	        base_cols = ("id, template_id, audience_user_id, subject, "
246	                     "body_rendered, attempts")
247	        cap = self._ENQUEUE_REPLAY_MAX_ATTEMPTS
248	        if force:
249	            rows = conn.execute(
250	                f"SELECT {base_cols} FROM notif_enqueue_failures "
251	                "WHERE replayed_at IS NULL AND dead_at IS NULL "
252	                "AND attempts < ?",
253	                (cap,)).fetchall()
254	        else:
255	            cutoff = (datetime.utcnow()
256	                      - timedelta(seconds=config.NOTIF_RETRY_INTERVAL_SECONDS)
257	                      ).isoformat(timespec="seconds")
258	            rows = conn.execute(
259	                f"SELECT {base_cols} FROM notif_enqueue_failures "
260	                "WHERE replayed_at IS NULL AND dead_at IS NULL "
261	                "AND attempts < ? AND created_at <= ?",
262	                (cap, cutoff)).fetchall()
263	        replayed = 0
264	        for r in rows:
265	            attempt_no = (r["attempts"] or 0) + 1
266	            try:
267	                with db.transaction() as conn:
268	                    conn.execute(
269	                        """INSERT INTO notif_messages(template_id, audience_user_id,
270	                              subject, body_rendered, status)
271	                           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'queued')""",
272	                        (r["template_id"], r["audience_user_id"],
273	                         r["subject"], r["body_rendered"]))
274	                    conn.execute(
275	                        "UPDATE notif_enqueue_failures SET "
276	                        "replayed_at=datetime('now'), attempts=? "
277	                        "WHERE id=?", (attempt_no, r["id"]))
278	                replayed += 1
279	            except Exception:
280	                # Bump the attempt counter even on failure so the cap is
281	                # enforced; mark dead once we've exhausted the budget.
282	                try:
283	                    with db.transaction() as conn:
284	                        if attempt_no >= cap:
285	                            conn.execute(
286	                                "UPDATE notif_enqueue_failures SET "
287	                                "attempts=?, dead_at=datetime('now') "
288	                                "WHERE id=?", (attempt_no, r["id"]))
289	                        else:
290	                            conn.execute(
291	                                "UPDATE notif_enqueue_failures SET "
292	                                "attempts=? WHERE id=?",
293	                                (attempt_no, r["id"]))
294	                except Exception:
295	                    pass
296	                continue
297	        return replayed
298	
299	
300	# ---- template rendering --------------------------------------------------
301	
302	def render(text: str, variables: dict[str, str]) -> str:
303	    def repl(m: re.Match) -> str:
304	        return str(variables.get(m.group(1), ""))
305	    return _VAR_RE.sub(repl, text)
306	
307	
308	# ---- trigger-rule wiring -------------------------------------------------
309	
310	def install_trigger_handlers(svc: NotificationService) -> None:
311	    """Subscribe to events on the bus and enqueue messages per rule.
312	
313	    Enqueue is single-shot: a single INSERT is attempted with a short
314	    SQLite-busy retry budget. Any persistent failure is recorded in
315	    ``notif_enqueue_failures`` and re-attempted by the dispatcher on the
316	    standard ``config.NOTIF_RETRY_INTERVAL_SECONDS`` (5 minute) cadence —
317	    matching the spec, instead of blocking the event-bus thread for minutes.
318	    """
319	    import sqlite3
320	    import time
321	
322	    # Tight in-process retry budget for transient SQLite "database is locked"
323	    # contention only — measured in milliseconds, not minutes. Persistent
324	    # failures escalate to ``notif_enqueue_failures`` so the dispatcher can
325	    # replay on the 5-minute cadence.
326	    _LOCK_RETRY_MAX = 3
327	    _LOCK_RETRY_BACKOFF_SECS = 0.05
328	
329	    def _insert_with_retry(template_id: int, uid: int, subject: str,
330	                            body: str, event_name: str) -> None:
331	        last_err: Exception | None = None
332	        # First attempt scheduled_for=NULL (deliver ASAP). Failed inserts go
333	        # to the failures table with a scheduled_for 5 minutes in the future
334	        # so retry_failed() / drain_queue() pick them up on the standard
335	        # cadence.
336	        for attempt in range(1, _LOCK_RETRY_MAX + 1):
337	            try:
338	                with db.transaction() as conn:
339	                    conn.execute(
340	                        """INSERT INTO notif_messages(template_id, audience_user_id,
341	                              subject, body_rendered, status, attempts,
342	                              last_attempt_at)
343	                           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'queued', 0, datetime('now'))""",
344	                        (template_id, uid, subject, body))
345	                return
346	            except sqlite3.OperationalError as e:
347	                last_err = e
348	                time.sleep(_LOCK_RETRY_BACKOFF_SECS * attempt)
349	            except Exception as e:
350	                last_err = e
351	                break
352	        # Persist failure so the operator sees + can replay.
353	        try:
354	            with db.transaction() as conn:
355	                conn.execute(
356	                    """INSERT INTO notif_enqueue_failures(event_name, template_id,
357	                          audience_user_id, subject, body_rendered, error,
358	                          created_at)
359	                       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))""",
360	                    (event_name, template_id, uid, subject, body,
361	                     repr(last_err)))
362	        except Exception:
363	            # Last-ditch: log to audit (audit writes are independent of the
364	            # notif tables, so a notif-table outage does not block this).
365	            try:
366	                audit.record(0, "notif_enqueue", uid, "fail_persist",
367	                             {"event": event_name, "error": repr(last_err)})
368	            except Exception:
369	                pass
370	
371	    def make_handler(event_name: str):
372	        def handler(payload: dict) -> None:
373	            conn = db.get_connection()
374	            rules = conn.execute(
375	                "SELECT r.id, r.template_id, r.audience_query, t.name AS tname "
376	                "FROM notif_rules r JOIN notif_templates t ON t.id=r.template_id "
377	                "WHERE r.enabled=1 AND r.kind='trigger' AND r.event_name=?",
378	                (event_name,)).fetchall()
379	            for rule in rules:
380	                aq = json.loads(rule["audience_query"] or "{}")
381	                user_ids = _resolve_audience(aq)
382	                if not user_ids:
383	                    continue
384	                vars_ = _payload_to_vars(payload)
385	                subject = render(_template_subject(rule["template_id"]), vars_)
386	                body = render(_template_body(rule["template_id"]), vars_)
387	                for uid in user_ids:
388	                    _insert_with_retry(rule["template_id"], uid, subject,
389	                                       body, event_name)
390	        return handler
391	
392	    for ev in (events.BED_ASSIGNED, events.BED_VACATED, events.BED_TRANSFERRED,
393	               events.RESOURCE_PUBLISHED, events.RESOURCE_UNPUBLISHED,
394	               events.RESOURCE_HELD, events.CASE_SUBMITTED, events.CASE_DECIDED,
395	               events.VIOLATION_OPENED, events.VIOLATION_RESOLVED):
396	        events.bus.subscribe(ev, make_handler(ev))
397	
398	
399	def _resolve_audience(aq: dict) -> list[int]:
400	    conn = db.get_connection()
401	    if not aq:
402	        return [r["id"] for r in conn.execute("SELECT id FROM users WHERE disabled=0")]
403	    role = aq.get("role")
404	    if role:
405	        # Members of the named role *plus* all system administrators (admins
406	        # see everything for operational visibility).
407	        rows = conn.execute(
408	            "SELECT DISTINCT u.id FROM users u "
409	            "JOIN user_roles ur ON ur.user_id=u.id "
410	            "JOIN roles r ON r.id=ur.role_id "
411	            "WHERE u.disabled=0 AND r.code IN (?, 'system_admin')",
412	            (role,)).fetchall()
413	        return [r["id"] for r in rows]
414	    if "user_id" in aq:
415	        return [int(aq["user_id"])]
416	    return []
417	
418	
419	def _template_subject(tid: int) -> str:
420	    return db.get_connection().execute(
421	        "SELECT subject FROM notif_templates WHERE id=?", (tid,)).fetchone()["subject"]
422	
423	
424	def _template_body(tid: int) -> str:
425	    return db.get_connection().execute(
426	        "SELECT body FROM notif_templates WHERE id=?", (tid,)).fetchone()["body"]
427	
428	
429	def _cron_matches(spec: str | None, when: datetime) -> bool:
430	    """Tiny cron-subset matcher: 'M H DOM MON DOW' with `*`, lists `1,5`,
431	    and ranges `1-5`. Sufficient for "daily at 7:00" and weekday digests.
432	    """
433	    if not spec:
434	        return False
435	    parts = spec.split()
436	    if len(parts) != 5:
437	        return False
438	    minute, hour, dom, mon, dow = parts
439	    fields = [
440	        (minute, when.minute, 0, 59),
441	        (hour,   when.hour,   0, 23),
442	        (dom,    when.day,    1, 31),
443	        (mon,    when.month,  1, 12),
444	        (dow,    when.isoweekday() % 7, 0, 6),  # cron Sun=0
445	    ]
446	    for raw, value, lo, hi in fields:
447	        if not _cron_field_matches(raw, value, lo, hi):
448	            return False
449	    return True
450	
451	
452	def _cron_field_matches(raw: str, value: int, lo: int, hi: int) -> bool:
453	    if raw == "*":
454	        return True
455	    for token in raw.split(","):
456	        token = token.strip()
457	        if "-" in token:
458	            try:
459	                a, b = (int(x) for x in token.split("-", 1))
460	            except ValueError:
461	                return False
462	            if a <= value <= b:
463	                return True
464	        else:
465	            try:
466	                if int(token) == value:
467	                    return True
468	            except ValueError:
469	                return False
470	    return False
471	
472	
473	def _payload_to_vars(payload: dict) -> dict[str, str]:
474	    vars_: dict[str, str] = {"Today": datetime.utcnow().strftime("%m/%d/%Y")}
475	    for k in ("StudentName", "Dorm", "Room", "Bed", "EffectiveDate",
476	              "ResourceTitle", "EmployerName", "Operator"):
477	        if k in payload:
478	            vars_[k] = str(payload[k])
479	    if "name" in payload and "StudentName" not in vars_:
480	        vars_["StudentName"] = str(payload["name"])
481	    if "operator" in payload:
482	        vars_["Operator"] = str(payload["operator"])
483	    if "effective_date" in payload:
484	        try:
485	            d = datetime.fromisoformat(payload["effective_date"])
486	            vars_["EffectiveDate"] = d.strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
487	        except Exception:
488	            vars_["EffectiveDate"] = str(payload["effective_date"])
489	    return vars_
490

file.content:
"""Notification & in-app messaging."""
from __future__ import annotations
import json
import re
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

from .. import audit, config, db, events
from ..models import NotificationMessage
from ..permissions import Session, requires
from .auth import BizError


_VAR_RE = re.compile(r"\{([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\}")


class NotificationService:

    # ---- templates --------------------------------------------------------

    def list_templates(self, session: Session) -> list[dict]:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        return [dict(r) for r in conn.execute(
            "SELECT id, name, subject, body, variables_json FROM notif_templates "
            "ORDER BY name")]

    @requires("notification.admin")
    def upsert_template(self, session: Session, name: str, subject: str,
                        body: str) -> int:
        variables = sorted(set(_VAR_RE.findall(subject + " " + body)))
        unknown = [v for v in variables if v not in config.TEMPLATE_VARIABLES]
        if unknown:
            audit.record(session.user_id, "notif_template", name,
                         "warn_unknown_vars", {"unknown": unknown})
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            existing = conn.execute(
                "SELECT id FROM notif_templates WHERE name=?", (name,)).fetchone()
            vj = json.dumps(variables)
            if existing:
                conn.execute(
                    "UPDATE notif_templates SET subject=?, body=?, variables_json=? "
                    "WHERE id=?", (subject, body, vj, existing["id"]))
                tid = existing["id"]
            else:
                cur = conn.execute(
                    "INSERT INTO notif_templates(name, subject, body, variables_json) "
                    "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)", (name, subject, body, vj))
                tid = cur.lastrowid
        audit.record(session.user_id, "notif_template", tid, "upsert",
                     {"name": name})
        return tid

    # ---- rules ------------------------------------------------------------

    def list_rules(self, session: Session) -> list[dict]:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        return [dict(r) for r in conn.execute(
            "SELECT id, name, kind, event_name, cron_spec, template_id, "
            "audience_query, enabled FROM notif_rules ORDER BY name")]

    @requires("notification.admin")
    def set_rule_enabled(self, session: Session, rule_id: int, enabled: bool) -> None:
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            conn.execute("UPDATE notif_rules SET enabled=? WHERE id=?",
                         (1 if enabled else 0, rule_id))
        audit.record(session.user_id, "notif_rule", rule_id, "set_enabled",
                     {"enabled": enabled})

    # ---- direct enqueue --------------------------------------------------

    def enqueue(self, session: Session, *, template_name: str,
                audience_user_ids: list[int], variables: dict[str, str],
                scheduled_for: datetime | None = None) -> list[int]:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        tpl = conn.execute(
            "SELECT id, subject, body FROM notif_templates WHERE name=?",
            (template_name,)).fetchone()
        if not tpl:
            raise BizError("TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND", "Unknown template.")
        subject = render(tpl["subject"], variables)
        body = render(tpl["body"], variables)
        msg_ids: list[int] = []
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            for uid in audience_user_ids:
                cur = conn.execute(
                    """INSERT INTO notif_messages(template_id, audience_user_id,
                          subject, body_rendered, status, scheduled_for)
                       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'queued', ?)""",
                    (tpl["id"], uid, subject, body,
                     scheduled_for.isoformat() if scheduled_for else None))
                msg_ids.append(cur.lastrowid)
        return msg_ids

    # ---- inbox ------------------------------------------------------------

    def inbox(self, session: Session, *, only_unread: bool = False,
              limit: int = 100) -> list[NotificationMessage]:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        sql = """SELECT m.id, t.name AS tname, m.subject, m.body_rendered,
                         m.status, m.attempts, m.scheduled_for, m.created_at, m.read_at
                  FROM notif_messages m JOIN notif_templates t ON t.id = m.template_id
                  WHERE m.audience_user_id = ? AND m.status='delivered'"""
        args = [session.user_id]
        if only_unread:
            sql += " AND m.read_at IS NULL"
        sql += " ORDER BY m.created_at DESC LIMIT ?"
        args.append(limit)
        rows = conn.execute(sql, args).fetchall()
        return [NotificationMessage(
            id=r["id"], template_name=r["tname"], subject=r["subject"],
            body=r["body_rendered"], status=r["status"], attempts=r["attempts"],
            scheduled_for=r["scheduled_for"], created_at=r["created_at"],
            read_at=r["read_at"]) for r in rows]

    def unread_count(self, session: Session) -> int:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        return conn.execute(
            "SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM notif_messages WHERE audience_user_id=? "
            "AND status='delivered' AND read_at IS NULL",
            (session.user_id,)).fetchone()["n"]

    def mark_read(self, session: Session, message_id: int) -> None:
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            conn.execute(
                "UPDATE notif_messages SET read_at=datetime('now') "
                "WHERE id=? AND audience_user_id=?",
                (message_id, session.user_id))

    # ---- dispatcher (called by worker) -----------------------------------

    def drain_queue(self) -> int:
        """Deliver due messages. Returns number delivered.

        Side-effect: any ``notif_enqueue_failures`` rows whose 5-minute retry
        window has elapsed are auto-replayed before draining, so the standard
        cadence applies even without an operator-driven retry.
        """
        self._auto_replay_failures()
        conn = db.get_connection()
        now_iso = datetime.utcnow().isoformat(timespec="seconds")
        rows = conn.execute(
            "SELECT id FROM notif_messages "
            "WHERE status='queued' AND (scheduled_for IS NULL OR scheduled_for <= ?)",
            (now_iso,)).fetchall()
        delivered = 0
        for r in rows:
            try:
                with db.transaction() as conn:
                    conn.execute(
                        "UPDATE notif_messages SET status='delivered', "
                        "attempts=attempts+1, last_attempt_at=datetime('now') "
                        "WHERE id=?", (r["id"],))
                delivered += 1
            except Exception:
                self._handle_failure(r["id"])
        return delivered

    def _handle_failure(self, msg_id: int) -> None:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        row = conn.execute(
            "SELECT attempts FROM notif_messages WHERE id=?", (msg_id,)).fetchone()
        if not row:
            return
        attempts = row["attempts"] + 1
        next_status = "dead" if attempts >= config.NOTIF_RETRY_LIMIT else "queued"
        next_for = (datetime.utcnow()
                    + timedelta(seconds=config.NOTIF_RETRY_INTERVAL_SECONDS)).isoformat()
        try:
            with db.transaction() as conn:
                conn.execute(
                    "UPDATE notif_messages SET attempts=?, status=?, "
                    "scheduled_for=?, last_attempt_at=datetime('now') WHERE id=?",
                    (attempts, next_status, next_for, msg_id))
        except Exception:
            pass

    def fire_scheduled_rules(self, now: datetime | None = None) -> int:
        """Evaluate all `kind='schedule'` rules whose cron spec matches *now*.

        Subset cron grammar: ``M H * * *`` (minute, hour, *, *, *) — fires
        once per matching minute. Last-fire timestamp lives in
        `notif_rule_runs` so a tick that lands inside the same minute does
        not re-fire. Returns number of rules fired.
        """
        ts = (now or datetime.now()).replace(second=0, microsecond=0)
        conn = db.get_connection()
        rows = conn.execute(
            "SELECT id, name, cron_spec, template_id, audience_query "
            "FROM notif_rules WHERE enabled=1 AND kind='schedule'").fetchall()
        fired = 0
        for r in rows:
            if not _cron_matches(r["cron_spec"], ts):
                continue
            last = conn.execute(
                "SELECT last_fired_at FROM notif_rule_runs WHERE rule_id=?",
                (r["id"],)).fetchone()
            this_min = ts.isoformat(timespec="minutes")
            if last and last["last_fired_at"] == this_min:
                continue
            audience = _resolve_audience(json.loads(r["audience_query"] or "{}"))
            if not audience:
                continue
            tpl_subject = _template_subject(r["template_id"])
            tpl_body = _template_body(r["template_id"])
            vars_ = {"Today": ts.strftime("%m/%d/%Y")}
            with db.transaction() as conn:
                for uid in audience:
                    conn.execute(
                        """INSERT INTO notif_messages(template_id, audience_user_id,
                              subject, body_rendered, status)
                           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'queued')""",
                        (r["template_id"], uid,
                         render(tpl_subject, vars_), render(tpl_body, vars_)))
                conn.execute(
                    "INSERT INTO notif_rule_runs(rule_id, last_fired_at) "
                    "VALUES (?, ?) ON CONFLICT(rule_id) DO UPDATE SET "
                    "last_fired_at=excluded.last_fired_at",
                    (r["id"], this_min))
            fired += 1
        return fired

    def retry_failed(self, session: Session) -> int:
        """Re-queue dead-letter rows AND replay any enqueue-stage failures."""
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            cur = conn.execute(
                "UPDATE notif_messages SET status='queued', attempts=0, "
                "scheduled_for=NULL WHERE status='dead'")
            requeued = cur.rowcount
        # Operator-triggered retry replays ALL outstanding enqueue failures
        # immediately (regardless of the 5-minute window).
        replayed = self._replay_enqueue_failures(force=True)
        return requeued + replayed

    def _auto_replay_failures(self) -> int:
        """Auto-replay only enqueue failures whose 5-minute window elapsed."""
        return self._replay_enqueue_failures(force=False)

    # Hard cap on enqueue-failure replay attempts. Matches the
    # delivery-side 3-attempt budget documented in design.md §7 so a
    # permanently-broken row is moved to a dead state instead of being
    # retried indefinitely on every dispatcher tick.
    _ENQUEUE_REPLAY_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3

    def _replay_enqueue_failures(self, *, force: bool) -> int:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        base_cols = ("id, template_id, audience_user_id, subject, "
                     "body_rendered, attempts")
        cap = self._ENQUEUE_REPLAY_MAX_ATTEMPTS
        if force:
            rows = conn.execute(
                f"SELECT {base_cols} FROM notif_enqueue_failures "
                "WHERE replayed_at IS NULL AND dead_at IS NULL "
                "AND attempts < ?",
                (cap,)).fetchall()
        else:
            cutoff = (datetime.utcnow()
                      - timedelta(seconds=config.NOTIF_RETRY_INTERVAL_SECONDS)
                      ).isoformat(timespec="seconds")
            rows = conn.execute(
                f"SELECT {base_cols} FROM notif_enqueue_failures "
                "WHERE replayed_at IS NULL AND dead_at IS NULL "
                "AND attempts < ? AND created_at <= ?",
                (cap, cutoff)).fetchall()
        replayed = 0
        for r in rows:
            attempt_no = (r["attempts"] or 0) + 1
            try:
                with db.transaction() as conn:
                    conn.execute(
                        """INSERT INTO notif_messages(template_id, audience_user_id,
                              subject, body_rendered, status)
                           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'queued')""",
                        (r["template_id"], r["audience_user_id"],
                         r["subject"], r["body_rendered"]))
                    conn.execute(
                        "UPDATE notif_enqueue_failures SET "
                        "replayed_at=datetime('now'), attempts=? "
                        "WHERE id=?", (attempt_no, r["id"]))
                replayed += 1
            except Exception:
                # Bump the attempt counter even on failure so the cap is
                # enforced; mark dead once we've exhausted the budget.
                try:
                    with db.transaction() as conn:
                        if attempt_no >= cap:
                            conn.execute(
                                "UPDATE notif_enqueue_failures SET "
                                "attempts=?, dead_at=datetime('now') "
                                "WHERE id=?", (attempt_no, r["id"]))
                        else:
                            conn.execute(
                                "UPDATE notif_enqueue_failures SET "
                                "attempts=? WHERE id=?",
                                (attempt_no, r["id"]))
                except Exception:
                    pass
                continue
        return replayed


# ---- template rendering --------------------------------------------------

def render(text: str, variables: dict[str, str]) -> str:
    def repl(m: re.Match) -> str:
        return str(variables.get(m.group(1), ""))
    return _VAR_RE.sub(repl, text)


# ---- trigger-rule wiring -------------------------------------------------

def install_trigger_handlers(svc: NotificationService) -> None:
    """Subscribe to events on the bus and enqueue messages per rule.

    Enqueue is single-shot: a single INSERT is attempted with a short
    SQLite-busy retry budget. Any persistent failure is recorded in
    ``notif_enqueue_failures`` and re-attempted by the dispatcher on the
    standard ``config.NOTIF_RETRY_INTERVAL_SECONDS`` (5 minute) cadence —
    matching the spec, instead of blocking the event-bus thread for minutes.
    """
    import sqlite3
    import time

    # Tight in-process retry budget for transient SQLite "database is locked"
    # contention only — measured in milliseconds, not minutes. Persistent
    # failures escalate to ``notif_enqueue_failures`` so the dispatcher can
    # replay on the 5-minute cadence.
    _LOCK_RETRY_MAX = 3
    _LOCK_RETRY_BACKOFF_SECS = 0.05

    def _insert_with_retry(template_id: int, uid: int, subject: str,
                            body: str, event_name: str) -> None:
        last_err: Exception | None = None
        # First attempt scheduled_for=NULL (deliver ASAP). Failed inserts go
        # to the failures table with a scheduled_for 5 minutes in the future
        # so retry_failed() / drain_queue() pick them up on the standard
        # cadence.
        for attempt in range(1, _LOCK_RETRY_MAX + 1):
            try:
                with db.transaction() as conn:
                    conn.execute(
                        """INSERT INTO notif_messages(template_id, audience_user_id,
                              subject, body_rendered, status, attempts,
                              last_attempt_at)
                           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'queued', 0, datetime('now'))""",
                        (template_id, uid, subject, body))
                return
            except sqlite3.OperationalError as e:
                last_err = e
                time.sleep(_LOCK_RETRY_BACKOFF_SECS * attempt)
            except Exception as e:
                last_err = e
                break
        # Persist failure so the operator sees + can replay.
        try:
            with db.transaction() as conn:
                conn.execute(
                    """INSERT INTO notif_enqueue_failures(event_name, template_id,
                          audience_user_id, subject, body_rendered, error,
                          created_at)
                       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))""",
                    (event_name, template_id, uid, subject, body,
                     repr(last_err)))
        except Exception:
            # Last-ditch: log to audit (audit writes are independent of the
            # notif tables, so a notif-table outage does not block this).
            try:
                audit.record(0, "notif_enqueue", uid, "fail_persist",
                             {"event": event_name, "error": repr(last_err)})
            except Exception:
                pass

    def make_handler(event_name: str):
        def handler(payload: dict) -> None:
            conn = db.get_connection()
            rules = conn.execute(
                "SELECT r.id, r.template_id, r.audience_query, t.name AS tname "
                "FROM notif_rules r JOIN notif_templates t ON t.id=r.template_id "
                "WHERE r.enabled=1 AND r.kind='trigger' AND r.event_name=?",
                (event_name,)).fetchall()
            for rule in rules:
                aq = json.loads(rule["audience_query"] or "{}")
                user_ids = _resolve_audience(aq)
                if not user_ids:
                    continue
                vars_ = _payload_to_vars(payload)
                subject = render(_template_subject(rule["template_id"]), vars_)
                body = render(_template_body(rule["template_id"]), vars_)
                for uid in user_ids:
                    _insert_with_retry(rule["template_id"], uid, subject,
                                       body, event_name)
        return handler

    for ev in (events.BED_ASSIGNED, events.BED_VACATED, events.BED_TRANSFERRED,
               events.RESOURCE_PUBLISHED, events.RESOURCE_UNPUBLISHED,
               events.RESOURCE_HELD, events.CASE_SUBMITTED, events.CASE_DECIDED,
               events.VIOLATION_OPENED, events.VIOLATION_RESOLVED):
        events.bus.subscribe(ev, make_handler(ev))


def _resolve_audience(aq: dict) -> list[int]:
    conn = db.get_connection()
    if not aq:
        return [r["id"] for r in conn.execute("SELECT id FROM users WHERE disabled=0")]
    role = aq.get("role")
    if role:
        # Members of the named role *plus* all system administrators (admins
        # see everything for operational visibility).
        rows = conn.execute(
            "SELECT DISTINCT u.id FROM users u "
            "JOIN user_roles ur ON ur.user_id=u.id "
            "JOIN roles r ON r.id=ur.role_id "
            "WHERE u.disabled=0 AND r.code IN (?, 'system_admin')",
            (role,)).fetchall()
        return [r["id"] for r in rows]
    if "user_id" in aq:
        return [int(aq["user_id"])]
    return []


def _template_subject(tid: int) -> str:
    return db.get_connection().execute(
        "SELECT subject FROM notif_templates WHERE id=?", (tid,)).fetchone()["subject"]


def _template_body(tid: int) -> str:
    return db.get_connection().execute(
        "SELECT body FROM notif_templates WHERE id=?", (tid,)).fetchone()["body"]


def _cron_matches(spec: str | None, when: datetime) -> bool:
    """Tiny cron-subset matcher: 'M H DOM MON DOW' with `*`, lists `1,5`,
    and ranges `1-5`. Sufficient for "daily at 7:00" and weekday digests.
    """
    if not spec:
        return False
    parts = spec.split()
    if len(parts) != 5:
        return False
    minute, hour, dom, mon, dow = parts
    fields = [
        (minute, when.minute, 0, 59),
        (hour,   when.hour,   0, 23),
        (dom,    when.day,    1, 31),
        (mon,    when.month,  1, 12),
        (dow,    when.isoweekday() % 7, 0, 6),  # cron Sun=0
    ]
    for raw, value, lo, hi in fields:
        if not _cron_field_matches(raw, value, lo, hi):
            return False
    return True


def _cron_field_matches(raw: str, value: int, lo: int, hi: int) -> bool:
    if raw == "*":
        return True
    for token in raw.split(","):
        token = token.strip()
        if "-" in token:
            try:
                a, b = (int(x) for x in token.split("-", 1))
            except ValueError:
                return False
            if a <= value <= b:
                return True
        else:
            try:
                if int(token) == value:
                    return True
            except ValueError:
                return False
    return False


def _payload_to_vars(payload: dict) -> dict[str, str]:
    vars_: dict[str, str] = {"Today": datetime.utcnow().strftime("%m/%d/%Y")}
    for k in ("StudentName", "Dorm", "Room", "Bed", "EffectiveDate",
              "ResourceTitle", "EmployerName", "Operator"):
        if k in payload:
            vars_[k] = str(payload[k])
    if "name" in payload and "StudentName" not in vars_:
        vars_["StudentName"] = str(payload["name"])
    if "operator" in payload:
        vars_["Operator"] = str(payload["operator"])
    if "effective_date" in payload:
        try:
            d = datetime.fromisoformat(payload["effective_date"])
            vars_["EffectiveDate"] = d.strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
        except Exception:
            vars_["EffectiveDate"] = str(payload["effective_date"])
    return vars_

1	"""Universal search across students, resources, employers, and cases."""
2	from __future__ import annotations
3	import json
4	
5	from .. import config, db
6	from ..models import SearchHit
7	from ..permissions import Session
8	
9	try:
10	    from rapidfuzz import fuzz
11	    HAVE_FUZZ = True
12	except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
13	    HAVE_FUZZ = False
14	
15	
16	class SearchService:
17	
18	    _READ_PERMS = ("student.write", "student.import", "student.pii.read",
19	                   "housing.write", "housing.read",
20	                   "resource.write", "resource.publish", "resource.read",
21	                   "compliance.review", "compliance.violation",
22	                   "compliance.evidence", "compliance.action",
23	                   "catalog.write", "catalog.review", "catalog.publish",
24	                   "system.admin")
25	
26	    def global_search(self, session: Session, query: str, *,
27	                      types: set[str] | None = None,
28	                      fuzzy: bool = True, limit: int = 20,
29	                      include_hidden: bool = False) -> list[SearchHit]:
30	        if not session.has_any(self._READ_PERMS):
31	            from ..permissions import PermissionDenied
32	            raise PermissionDenied("search.read")
33	        types = types or {"student", "resource", "employer", "case"}
34	        expanded = self._expand_synonyms(query)
35	        hits: list[SearchHit] = []
36	        if "student" in types:
37	            hits.extend(self._search_students(expanded))
38	        if "resource" in types:
39	            hits.extend(self._search_resources(expanded))
40	        if "employer" in types:
41	            hits.extend(self._search_employers(expanded, include_hidden=include_hidden))
42	        if "case" in types:
43	            hits.extend(self._search_cases(expanded))
44	        if fuzzy and HAVE_FUZZ:
45	            for h in hits:
46	                h.score = max(h.score,
47	                              fuzz.token_set_ratio(query.lower(), h.title.lower()))
48	            hits = [h for h in hits if h.score >= config.FUZZY_THRESHOLD] or hits
49	        hits.sort(key=lambda h: h.score, reverse=True)
50	        return hits[:limit]
51	
52	    def _expand_synonyms(self, query: str) -> str:
53	        conn = db.get_connection()
54	        parts = []
55	        for tok in query.split():
56	            base = tok.lower()
57	            alts = [r["alt_term"] for r in conn.execute(
58	                "SELECT alt_term FROM synonyms WHERE term=?", (base,))]
59	            parts.append("(" + " OR ".join([base] + alts) + ")" if alts else tok)
60	        return " ".join(parts)
61	
62	    def _search_students(self, q: str) -> list[SearchHit]:
63	        conn = db.get_connection()
64	        try:
65	            rows = conn.execute(
66	                "SELECT s.id, s.full_name, s.student_id_ext, s.college, "
67	                "       students_fts.rank "
68	                "FROM students_fts JOIN students s ON s.id = students_fts.rowid "
69	                "WHERE students_fts MATCH ? LIMIT 50", (q,)).fetchall()
70	        except Exception:
71	            rows = conn.execute(
72	                "SELECT id, full_name, student_id_ext, college, 0 AS rank "
73	                "FROM students WHERE full_name LIKE ? OR student_id_ext LIKE ? LIMIT 50",
74	                (f"%{q}%", f"%{q}%")).fetchall()
75	        return [SearchHit(
76	            entity_type="student", entity_id=r["id"],
77	            title=r["full_name"], subtitle=f"{r['student_id_ext']} · {r['college'] or ''}",
78	            score=80.0, open_action="open_student") for r in rows]
79	
80	    def _search_resources(self, q: str) -> list[SearchHit]:
81	        conn = db.get_connection()
82	        try:
83	            rows = conn.execute(
84	                "SELECT r.id, r.title, r.status FROM resources_fts "
85	                "JOIN resources r ON r.id = resources_fts.rowid "
86	                "WHERE resources_fts MATCH ? LIMIT 50", (q,)).fetchall()
87	        except Exception:
88	            rows = conn.execute(
89	                "SELECT id, title, status FROM resources WHERE title LIKE ? LIMIT 50",
90	                (f"%{q}%",)).fetchall()
91	        return [SearchHit(entity_type="resource", entity_id=r["id"],
92	                          title=r["title"], subtitle=f"status: {r['status']}",
93	                          score=80.0, open_action="open_resource") for r in rows]
94	
95	    def _search_employers(self, q: str, include_hidden: bool = False) -> list[SearchHit]:
96	        conn = db.get_connection()
97	        try:
98	            rows = conn.execute(
99	                "SELECT e.id, e.name, e.status FROM employers_fts "
100	                "JOIN employers e ON e.id = employers_fts.rowid "
101	                "WHERE employers_fts MATCH ? LIMIT 50", (q,)).fetchall()
102	        except Exception:
103	            rows = conn.execute(
104	                "SELECT id, name, status FROM employers WHERE name LIKE ? LIMIT 50",
105	                (f"%{q}%",)).fetchall()
106	        # Default search excludes throttled / taken-down / suspended employers
107	        # AND any employer carrying an active violation_action of those types.
108	        if not include_hidden:
109	            hidden_status = {"throttled", "taken_down", "suspended"}
110	            hidden_ids = {r["employer_id"] for r in conn.execute(
111	                "SELECT DISTINCT employer_id FROM violation_actions "
112	                "WHERE revoked_at IS NULL "
113	                "AND (ends_at IS NULL OR ends_at > datetime('now')) "
114	                "AND action IN ('takedown','suspend','throttle')")}
115	            rows = [r for r in rows
116	                    if r["status"] not in hidden_status
117	                    and r["id"] not in hidden_ids]
118	        return [SearchHit(entity_type="employer", entity_id=r["id"],
119	                          title=r["name"], subtitle=f"status: {r['status']}",
120	                          score=80.0, open_action="open_employer") for r in rows]
121	
122	    def _search_cases(self, q: str) -> list[SearchHit]:
123	        conn = db.get_connection()
124	        rows = conn.execute(
125	            "SELECT c.id, e.name, c.kind, c.state FROM employer_cases c "
126	            "JOIN employers e ON e.id=c.employer_id "
127	            "WHERE e.name LIKE ? OR c.notes LIKE ? LIMIT 50",
128	            (f"%{q}%", f"%{q}%")).fetchall()

130	                          title=f"{r['name']} — {r['kind']}",
131	                          subtitle=f"state: {r['state']}",
132	                          score=75.0, open_action="open_case") for r in rows]

134	    # ---- saved searches ---------------------------------------------------
135

137	                    query: dict) -> int:

141	                "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 0)",

"""Universal search across students, resources, employers, and cases."""
from __future__ import annotations
import json

from .. import config, db
from ..models import SearchHit
from ..permissions import Session

try:
    from rapidfuzz import fuzz
    HAVE_FUZZ = True
except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
    HAVE_FUZZ = False


class SearchService:

    _READ_PERMS = ("student.write", "student.import", "student.pii.read",
                   "housing.write", "housing.read",
                   "resource.write", "resource.publish", "resource.read",
                   "compliance.review", "compliance.violation",
                   "compliance.evidence", "compliance.action",
                   "catalog.write", "catalog.review", "catalog.publish",
                   "system.admin")

    def global_search(self, session: Session, query: str, *,
                      types: set[str] | None = None,
                      fuzzy: bool = True, limit: int = 20,
                      include_hidden: bool = False) -> list[SearchHit]:
        if not session.has_any(self._READ_PERMS):
            from ..permissions import PermissionDenied
            raise PermissionDenied("search.read")
        types = types or {"student", "resource", "employer", "case"}
        expanded = self._expand_synonyms(query)
        hits: list[SearchHit] = []
        if "student" in types:
            hits.extend(self._search_students(expanded))
        if "resource" in types:
            hits.extend(self._search_resources(expanded))
        if "employer" in types:
            hits.extend(self._search_employers(expanded, include_hidden=include_hidden))
        if "case" in types:
            hits.extend(self._search_cases(expanded))
        if fuzzy and HAVE_FUZZ:
            for h in hits:
                h.score = max(h.score,
                              fuzz.token_set_ratio(query.lower(), h.title.lower()))
            hits = [h for h in hits if h.score >= config.FUZZY_THRESHOLD] or hits
        hits.sort(key=lambda h: h.score, reverse=True)
        return hits[:limit]

    def _expand_synonyms(self, query: str) -> str:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        parts = []
        for tok in query.split():
            base = tok.lower()
            alts = [r["alt_term"] for r in conn.execute(
                "SELECT alt_term FROM synonyms WHERE term=?", (base,))]
            parts.append("(" + " OR ".join([base] + alts) + ")" if alts else tok)
        return " ".join(parts)

    def _search_students(self, q: str) -> list[SearchHit]:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        try:
            rows = conn.execute(
                "SELECT s.id, s.full_name, s.student_id_ext, s.college, "
                "       students_fts.rank "
                "FROM students_fts JOIN students s ON s.id = students_fts.rowid "
                "WHERE students_fts MATCH ? LIMIT 50", (q,)).fetchall()
        except Exception:
            rows = conn.execute(
                "SELECT id, full_name, student_id_ext, college, 0 AS rank "
                "FROM students WHERE full_name LIKE ? OR student_id_ext LIKE ? LIMIT 50",
                (f"%{q}%", f"%{q}%")).fetchall()
        return [SearchHit(
            entity_type="student", entity_id=r["id"],
            title=r["full_name"], subtitle=f"{r['student_id_ext']} · {r['college'] or ''}",
            score=80.0, open_action="open_student") for r in rows]

    def _search_resources(self, q: str) -> list[SearchHit]:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        try:
            rows = conn.execute(
                "SELECT r.id, r.title, r.status FROM resources_fts "
                "JOIN resources r ON r.id = resources_fts.rowid "
                "WHERE resources_fts MATCH ? LIMIT 50", (q,)).fetchall()
        except Exception:
            rows = conn.execute(
                "SELECT id, title, status FROM resources WHERE title LIKE ? LIMIT 50",
                (f"%{q}%",)).fetchall()
        return [SearchHit(entity_type="resource", entity_id=r["id"],
                          title=r["title"], subtitle=f"status: {r['status']}",
                          score=80.0, open_action="open_resource") for r in rows]

    def _search_employers(self, q: str, include_hidden: bool = False) -> list[SearchHit]:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        try:
            rows = conn.execute(
                "SELECT e.id, e.name, e.status FROM employers_fts "
                "JOIN employers e ON e.id = employers_fts.rowid "
                "WHERE employers_fts MATCH ? LIMIT 50", (q,)).fetchall()
        except Exception:
            rows = conn.execute(
                "SELECT id, name, status FROM employers WHERE name LIKE ? LIMIT 50",
                (f"%{q}%",)).fetchall()
        # Default search excludes throttled / taken-down / suspended employers
        # AND any employer carrying an active violation_action of those types.
        if not include_hidden:
            hidden_status = {"throttled", "taken_down", "suspended"}
            hidden_ids = {r["employer_id"] for r in conn.execute(
                "SELECT DISTINCT employer_id FROM violation_actions "
                "WHERE revoked_at IS NULL "
                "AND (ends_at IS NULL OR ends_at > datetime('now')) "
                "AND action IN ('takedown','suspend','throttle')")}
            rows = [r for r in rows
                    if r["status"] not in hidden_status
                    and r["id"] not in hidden_ids]
        return [SearchHit(entity_type="employer", entity_id=r["id"],
                          title=r["name"], subtitle=f"status: {r['status']}",
                          score=80.0, open_action="open_employer") for r in rows]

    def _search_cases(self, q: str) -> list[SearchHit]:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        rows = conn.execute(
            "SELECT c.id, e.name, c.kind, c.state FROM employer_cases c "
            "JOIN employers e ON e.id=c.employer_id "
            "WHERE e.name LIKE ? OR c.notes LIKE ? LIMIT 50",
            (f"%{q}%", f"%{q}%")).fetchall()
        return [SearchHit(entity_type="case", entity_id=r["id"],
                          title=f"{r['name']} — {r['kind']}",
                          subtitle=f"state: {r['state']}",
                          score=75.0, open_action="open_case") for r in rows]

    # ---- saved searches ---------------------------------------------------

    def save_search(self, session: Session, name: str, scope: str,
                    query: dict) -> int:
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            cur = conn.execute(
                "INSERT INTO saved_searches(owner_id, name, scope, query_json, pinned) "
                "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 0)",
                (session.user_id, name, scope, json.dumps(query)))
        return cur.lastrowid

    def list_saved(self, session: Session) -> list[dict]:
        conn = db.get_connection()
        rows = conn.execute(
            "SELECT id, name, scope, query_json, pinned FROM saved_searches "
            "WHERE owner_id=? ORDER BY pinned DESC, name", (session.user_id,)).fetchall()
        out = []
        for r in rows:
            d = dict(r)
            d["query"] = json.loads(d.pop("query_json"))
            out.append(d)
        return out

    def pin(self, session: Session, search_id: int, pinned: bool) -> None:
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            conn.execute("UPDATE saved_searches SET pinned=? WHERE id=? AND owner_id=?",
                         (1 if pinned else 0, search_id, session.user_id))

    def delete_saved(self, session: Session, search_id: int) -> None:
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            conn.execute("DELETE FROM saved_searches WHERE id=? AND owner_id=?",
                         (search_id, session.user_id))

"""Workspace state and unsaved-draft checkpoints (every 60 s).

`workspace_state` holds one row per user describing the open tabs and
detached windows so the next session can restore them.

`draft_checkpoints` holds in-progress form data keyed by `(user_id, draft_key)`.
The UI calls `save_draft()` on a 60-second QTimer; on launch it calls
`load_draft()` and offers to restore.
"""
from __future__ import annotations
import json
from typing import Any

from .. import db
from ..permissions import Session


class CheckpointService:

    # ---- Workspace state ----------------------------------------------

    def save_workspace(self, session: Session, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            conn.execute(
                """INSERT INTO workspace_state(user_id, payload_json, saved_at)
                   VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))
                   ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO UPDATE SET
                        payload_json=excluded.payload_json,
                        saved_at=excluded.saved_at""",
                (session.user_id, json.dumps(payload, default=str)))

    def load_workspace(self, session: Session) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        r = db.get_connection().execute(
            "SELECT payload_json FROM workspace_state WHERE user_id=?",
            (session.user_id,)).fetchone()
        if not r:
            return None
        try:
            return json.loads(r["payload_json"])
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            return None

    # ---- Drafts -------------------------------------------------------

    def save_draft(self, session: Session, draft_key: str,
                   payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            conn.execute(
                """INSERT INTO draft_checkpoints(user_id, draft_key, payload_json, saved_at)
                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
                   ON CONFLICT(user_id, draft_key) DO UPDATE SET
                        payload_json=excluded.payload_json,
                        saved_at=excluded.saved_at""",
                (session.user_id, draft_key, json.dumps(payload, default=str)))

    def load_draft(self, session: Session,
                   draft_key: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        r = db.get_connection().execute(
            "SELECT payload_json FROM draft_checkpoints "
            "WHERE user_id=? AND draft_key=?",
            (session.user_id, draft_key)).fetchone()
        if not r:
            return None
        try:
            return json.loads(r["payload_json"])
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            return None

    def list_drafts(self, session: Session) -> list[dict]:
        rows = db.get_connection().execute(
            "SELECT draft_key, saved_at FROM draft_checkpoints "
            "WHERE user_id=? ORDER BY saved_at DESC",
            (session.user_id,)).fetchall()
        return [dict(r) for r in rows]

    def discard_draft(self, session: Session, draft_key: str) -> None:
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            conn.execute(
                "DELETE FROM draft_checkpoints WHERE user_id=? AND draft_key=?",
                (session.user_id, draft_key))

    def discard_all(self, session: Session) -> int:
        with db.transaction() as conn:
            cur = conn.execute(
                "DELETE FROM draft_checkpoints WHERE user_id=?",
                (session.user_id,))
        return cur.rowcount

content.content:
1	"""Offline signed update package import + rollback.
2	
3	Update package format (a single ZIP file):
4	    update.json        — {"version": "1.2.0", "files": [...], "notes": "..."}
5	    payload/...        — files copied into the install dir, mirroring layout
6	    update.json.sig    — RSA-PSS signature over update.json (DER bytes)
7	
8	The installer ships the public key at ``update_pubkey.pem`` in the data
9	directory. If `cryptography` is unavailable, signature verification falls
10	back to a SHA-256 fingerprint check and the row is recorded with
11	``signature_ok=0`` so operators can see the difference.
12	
13	Before applying, a snapshot of the current SQLite database is copied to
14	``snapshots/<applied_at>__crhgc.db``. ``rollback(package_id)`` restores
15	that snapshot and marks the row as rolled back. Rollback is reversible by
16	re-applying a newer package.
17	"""
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	import hashlib
20	import json
21	import shutil
22	import zipfile
23	from dataclasses import dataclass
24	from datetime import datetime
25	from pathlib import Path
26	
27	from .. import audit, config, db
28	from ..permissions import Session, requires
29	from .auth import BizError
30	
31	try:
32	    from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import hashes, serialization
33	    from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import padding
34	    HAVE_CRYPTO = True
35	except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
36	    HAVE_CRYPTO = False
37	
38	
39	def _default_install_dir() -> Path:
40	    """Best guess for the actual application install directory.
41	
42	    - When packaged with PyInstaller (``sys.frozen``), the installed binary
43	      lives next to its loader, so we use the executable's parent directory.
44	    - When running from a checkout, fall back to the repo root that contains
45	      the running ``main.py`` / ``backend/`` package.
46	    """
47	    import sys
48	    if getattr(sys, "frozen", False):
49	        return Path(sys.executable).resolve().parent
50	    return config.REPO_ROOT
51	
52	
53	@dataclass
54	class UpdatePackage:
55	    id: int
56	    version: str
57	    sha256: str
58	    signed_by: str | None
59	    signature_ok: bool
60	    applied_at: str
61	    rolled_back_at: str | None
62	    snapshot_path: str | None
63	    notes: str | None
64	
65	
66	class UpdaterService:
67	
68	    @requires("update.apply")
69	    def apply_package(self, session: Session,
70	                      zip_path: str | Path,
71	                      install_dir: str | Path | None = None) -> UpdatePackage:
72	        """Apply a signed update package.
73	
74	        Packages with a missing or invalid RSA-PSS signature are REJECTED
75	        with ``BizError(SIGNATURE_REQUIRED)``. There is intentionally no
76	        ``allow_unsigned`` override at any layer (backend or UI): tests
77	        that need to exercise the apply path must produce a properly
78	        signed package using the documented signing key. This eliminates
79	        the previous "audited test hook" that could be invoked from any
80	        caller holding the ``update.apply`` permission.
81	        """
82	        zp = Path(zip_path)
83	        if not zp.is_file():
84	            raise BizError("PACKAGE_MISSING", str(zp))
85	
86	        digest = self._sha256(zp)
87	        with zipfile.ZipFile(zp) as zf:
88	            try:
89	                manifest_bytes = zf.read("update.json")
90	            except KeyError:
91	                raise BizError("BAD_PACKAGE", "update.json missing from package")
92	            try:
93	                manifest = json.loads(manifest_bytes.decode("utf-8"))
94	            except (UnicodeDecodeError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:
95	                raise BizError("BAD_PACKAGE", f"manifest parse error: {e}")
96	            sig = None
97	            try:
98	                sig = zf.read("update.json.sig")
99	            except KeyError:
100	                pass
101	
102	            sig_ok, signer = self._verify_signature(manifest_bytes, sig)
103	            if not sig_ok:
104	                raise BizError(
105	                    "SIGNATURE_REQUIRED",
106	                    f"Package signature could not be verified "
107	                    f"({signer or 'no signature'}). Replace the public key "
108	                    f"at {config.update_signing_key_path()} with the "
109	                    "production key, or obtain a properly signed package "
110	                    "from the release pipeline.")
111	
112	            # Insert the package row FIRST so the snapshot includes the
113	            # provenance record. Rollback then restores a DB that still
114	            # remembers this apply.
115	            with db.transaction() as conn:
116	                cur = conn.execute(
117	                    """INSERT INTO update_packages(version, sha256, signed_by,
118	                            signature_ok, snapshot_path, notes)
119	                       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
120	                    (manifest.get("version", "?"), digest, signer,
121	                     1 if sig_ok else 0, "", manifest.get("notes")))
122	                pid = cur.lastrowid
123	
124	            snap = self._snapshot_db()
125	            with db.transaction() as conn:
126	                conn.execute(
127	                    "UPDATE update_packages SET snapshot_path=? WHERE id=?",
128	                    (str(snap), pid))
129	
130	            # Copy payload/ files into the install dir. When no caller-
131	            # supplied path is given, target the actual application install
132	            # directory (frozen exe parent or repo root) — NOT the per-user
133	            # data folder. The per-user folder used to be the default which
134	            # silently let updates land somewhere they would never be loaded.
135	            target_root = (Path(install_dir) if install_dir
136	                           else _default_install_dir())
137	            target_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
138	            for member in zf.namelist():
139	                if not member.startswith("payload/") or member.endswith("/"):
140	                    continue
141	                rel = member[len("payload/"):]
142	                # Reject path traversal.
143	                if rel.startswith("/") or ".." in Path(rel).parts:
144	                    raise BizError("BAD_PACKAGE",
145	                                   f"unsafe path in package: {member}")
146	                dest = target_root / rel
147	                dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
148	                with zf.open(member) as src, dest.open("wb") as out:
149	                    shutil.copyfileobj(src, out)
150	        audit.record(session.user_id, "update_package", pid, "apply",
151	                     {"version": manifest.get("version"),
152	                      "sha256": digest, "signature_ok": sig_ok,
153	                      "snapshot": str(snap)})
154	        return self.get(pid)
155	
156	    @requires("update.apply")
157	    def rollback(self, session: Session, package_id: int) -> None:
158	        conn = db.get_connection()
159	        r = conn.execute(
160	            "SELECT snapshot_path, rolled_back_at FROM update_packages WHERE id=?",
161	            (package_id,)).fetchone()
162	        if not r:
163	            raise BizError("PACKAGE_NOT_FOUND", str(package_id))
164	        if r["rolled_back_at"]:
165	            raise BizError("ALREADY_ROLLED_BACK", "package already rolled back")
166	        snap = Path(r["snapshot_path"]) if r["snapshot_path"] else None
167	        if not snap or not snap.is_file():
168	            raise BizError("SNAPSHOT_MISSING", "snapshot not available")
169	        # Take a *current* snapshot before restoring so this rollback is also
170	        # reversible.
171	        current = self._snapshot_db(prefix="pre_rollback")
172	        # Restore: snapshots are encrypted blobs in the same envelope format
173	        # as the at-rest DB. Replace the at-rest blob and reopen so the next
174	        # ``get_connection`` call deserializes from the restored state.
175	        from .. import db as _db
176	        _db.reset_connection()
177	        enc_target = config.db_path().with_suffix(config.db_path().suffix + ".enc")
178	        shutil.copy2(snap, enc_target)
179	        _db.get_connection()  # reopen (deserializes from enc_target)
180	        with db.transaction() as conn:
181	            conn.execute(
182	                "UPDATE update_packages SET rolled_back_at=datetime('now'), "
183	                "notes=COALESCE(notes,'')||char(10)||? WHERE id=?",
184	                (f"rolled back; pre-rollback snapshot at {current}", package_id))
185	        audit.record(session.user_id, "update_package", package_id, "rollback",
186	                     {"restored_from": str(snap),
187	                      "pre_rollback_snapshot": str(current)})
188	
189	    def list_packages(self) -> list[UpdatePackage]:
190	        rows = db.get_connection().execute(
191	            "SELECT * FROM update_packages ORDER BY applied_at DESC").fetchall()
192	        return [self._row(r) for r in rows]
193	
194	    def get(self, package_id: int) -> UpdatePackage:
195	        r = db.get_connection().execute(
196	            "SELECT * FROM update_packages WHERE id=?", (package_id,)).fetchone()
197	        if not r:
198	            raise BizError("PACKAGE_NOT_FOUND", str(package_id))
199	        return self._row(r)
200	
201	    # ---- Internals -----------------------------------------------------
202	
203	    def _row(self, r) -> UpdatePackage:
204	        return UpdatePackage(
